item.title: UK Supreme Court rules against Orsted in landmark case for renewables
item.description: Supreme Court favours narrow interpretation of what costs project developers can claim tax relief for, with implications for wind, solar and other industries

item.title: Why the US wind industry is caught in a polling paradox
item.description: Recent surveys indicate that Republicans support either renewable energy or fossil fuels depending on framing, with issues of affordability trumping the environment

item.title: UK energy minister hits back at Scottish leader in Mingyang row
item.description: Scotland’s First Minister Swinney had decried move by UK government bar turbines from China's Mingyang over national security concerns

item.title: Orsted braces for UK Supreme Court ruling with major implications for wind
item.description: Supreme Court ruling concerning four offshore wind farms will affect the tax payable for surveys during development of all kinds of renewable energy projects

item.title: Chinese government says UK Mingyang decision will damage relations
item.description: UK barred Mingyang turbines from offshore wind farms in British waters, citing concerns over national security

item.title: Equinor sells stake in renewables developer amid shift away from wind and solar
item.description: Oil and gas giant halves holding in Norwegian company after previously lowering capacity target for renewables

item.title: Nordex Q1 wind turbine orders fall while prices rise slightly
item.description: CEO Blanco sees 'healthy order pipeline' in Europea and North America for the full year

item.title: Chinese OEMs top wind turbine deliveries to dominate global market
item.description: China's manufacturers emphasised scale, but Western OEMs saw healthy boosts too by focusing on value and price discipline, said consultancy Wood Mackenzie

item.title: 'World's largest' wind plus solar plant now pumping power
item.description: Envison Energy says 6GW mega-plant, boasting a mix of wind farms and solar farms, is operating in remote mountainous northwest China

item.title: Six key claims in Iberdrola's lawsuit against GE Vernova
item.description: Spanish renewable energy giant Iberdrola has slammed GE Vernova's work on huge offshore wind project, claiming things started poorly and became ‘catastrophic’

item.title: Massive battery blaze raises new safety questions
item.description: More than 80 tonnes of batteries went up in flames in incident likely to turn new spotlight on industry's safety record

item.title: Iberdrola dispute with GE Vernova over US offshore wind farm erupts into open
item.description: Dispute stems from fallout of blade breaking off GE Vernova wind turbine at Vineyard Wind project in 2024, which led to huge replacement programme and extensive delays

item.title: Iberdrola dispute with GE Vernova over US offshore wind farm erupts into the open
item.description: Dispute stems from fallout of blade breaking off GE Vernova wind turbine at Vineyard Wind project in 2024, which led to huge replacement programme and extensive delays

item.title: Iberdrola wind farm hit by last minute stop work order – and not in the US this time
item.description: Spain's energy ministry halts nearly-complete Vestas-equipped project after failure to respond to appeals triggers automatic suspension

item.title: Deals of the Week: RWE, Nordex, TenneT and Galp
item.description: RWE wrapped up major sale of Swedish wind power assets, while Nordex announced two wind turbine orders and TenneT and National Grid entered major offshore grid deal

item.title: Solar project twice the size of Paris to produce green fuels
item.description: The 13.5GW of solar panels will be used to produce green methanol that will then be further processed into polyester

item.title: Trailblazing UK floating wind project sold for £1 after contract cancelled
item.description: Planned floating offshore wind farm ran into trouble due to significant increases in inflation and supply chain costs

item.title: Floater pioneer takes hands-on approach to solve the 'chicken and egg' problem
item.description: France's top floating wind technology sees calculated risk-taking and some firm choices as the route to serialised European production of floater

item.title: 'This war is a reckoning for our energy system – the green solution is clear'
item.description: Large scale wind and solar farms producing green hydrogen and ammonia are the only credible path to both fuel and climate security, writes Alex Tancock

item.title: The surprise market that's trouncing the EU on energy storage
item.description: Turkey has approved 33GW of battery storage projects, compared to around 12-13GW in Germany and Italy

item.title: Why Europe’s biggest power users are feeling frustrated
item.description: Flexibility and grid expansion are all very well, says European Metals chief James Watson, but not if his members end up paying

item.title: The business plan behind 'the world's biggest battery' – and its seeds at Tesla
item.description: Form Energy has set the energy storage industry buzzing with a trio of big deals in 2026 – its CEO tells Recharge that scale was always the plan

item.title: Deadline looms for Pentagon response on US wind permitting delays
item.description: The Defence Department is holding up 7.5GW of new capacity without explanation, renewables advocate American Clean Power Association said in a letter last month

item.title: US anti-offshore wind groups waffle on Trump oil & gas support
item.description: Industry opponents praise President for his relentless attacks but other actions including exempting offshore oil & gas from key environmental protections are raising concerns

item.title: Inside the 'baffling' mindset of US offshore wind opponents
item.description: Industry opponents praise President for his relentless attacks but other actions including exempting offshore oil & gas from key environmental protections are raising concerns

item.title: Democrats write to Total chief slamming 'outrageous' Trump offshore wind deal
item.description: House Democrats demand answers over how deal to quit US offshore wind farm projects and not come back was struck, and the legal basis for it

item.title: EU windfall tax could ‘endanger’ investments warns WindEurope
item.description: The European wind industry lobby thinks making interventions to the market is ‘unjustified’ because the current situation is not as severe as in 2022

item.title: How insurers adapted after 'death by a thousand cuts' from offshore wind
item.description: Insurers are now finding right balance after being burned by low cost, high frequency breakdowns, says head of renewables at Tokio Marine GX

item.title: Class action lawsuit filed against US battery firm
item.description: At least four law firms have issued alerts, alleging Eos Energy committed securities fraud

item.title: Pioneering UK floating wind project has contract terminated
item.description: UK chief of Mingyang says 'fact speaks for itself' after planned array it had preferred supplier agreement for has offtake deal terminated weeks after Chinese supplier barred from British projects
